title: Single Number
coreIdea: Find the element that appears exactly once while every other element appears twice, by XOR-ing all elements together, since XOR-ing a number with itself cancels to zero, leaving only the unique element.
recognitionCues: every element appears twice except one
asks for O(n) time and O(1) space, ruling out hashmap counting
approach: Initialize result to 0. XOR every element in the array into result. Pairs cancel out to 0 (a XOR a = 0), and XOR with 0 leaves a value unchanged, so the final result is the single unique element.
